items.firstComment: Amy Madigan as a young Gladys will flatten the eerie enigma into a tired origin saga — this prequel is already a misstep.

fb.description:
Zach Cregger is developing a prequel to his hit film Weapons, titled Gladys. The new film will focus on the origin story of Amy Madigan's character, Aunt Gladys. Cregger confirmed the script is in progress. What do you hope to see in the prequel? Let us know below!

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#ZachCregger #Gladys

items.firstComment: Method acting shouldn’t endanger basic sight and safety—talent stands without self-destruction.

fb.description:
John Leguizamo wore blind contact lenses for 12-14 hours daily while filming Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. He stated, "Not being able to see for 12 to 14 hours a day, like I couldn’t see my phone, I couldn’t read, I couldn’t call." Leguizamo had to be led to the bathroom and meals during filming. What do you think of this intense acting commitment? Share your thoughts below!

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#JohnLeguizamo #TheOdyssey

items.firstComment: The twins’ gun-toting prophecy lands as bold social critique, not edgy shock—anyone who thinks otherwise is defending respectability over truth in horror.

fb.description:
Mark H. Rapaport's new thriller 'Godhead' premiered at Fantasia Festival, featuring a priest and armed twins. The film's ending was reshaped into something horrifying by an actor's suggestion. What did you think of the film's tense, black-and-white style? Let us know in the comments!

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#FantasiaFestival #MarkHRapaport

items.firstComment: Julia Roberts narrating Leonard and Hungry Paul is peak prestige fluff — this slate feels more like vanity packaging than a bold strategy.

fb.description:
Wonder Project is expanding its international scripted programming with three new series from the U.K. and Ireland. The slate includes the Julia Roberts-narrated Leonard and Hungry Paul and the Russell T Davies-produced You & Me. What do you think of these new acquisitions?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#WonderProject #LeonardAndHungryPaul

items.firstComment: This cinematic rollout proves the Paris nights were legendary, not just another tour film.

fb.description:
Katy Perry's "The Lifetimes Tour – Live From Paris" concert film is expanding to 70 countries. Shot across two sold-out nights in Paris in November 2025, the film premiered at Tribeca in June 2026. Director Paul Dugdale used a 60-camera setup for the film. What do you think of this global release?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#KatyPerry #TribecaFestival
